title: Authorization matrix test
description: Build a role, owner, tenant, and state matrix before declaring access-control behavior correct.
body: Use when modifying object-level permissions, admin flows, tenant isolation, feature flags, or data visibility. Do not use for pure authentication flows that never access protected objects. Steps: list principals, resource ownership, tenant boundaries, resource states, and expected allow or deny result; implement focused tests for the highest-risk denies plus one allowed path; verify response bodies and logs do not leak hidden data. Validation signals include matrix coverage and explicit denied assertions. Risk boundary: ambiguous policy cells require human product/security input before code changes.
